[High] src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:516 — Interpolating user-controlled `cwd` into shell source permits command substitution/quote injection and bypasses policy scanning; retain `spawn(..., { cwd })`.

[High] src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/store.mjs:578 — Treating malformed session JSON as a valid empty session lets resume/update/close paths overwrite recoverable data instead of failing safely.

[Medium] src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:727 — The exit-code write is not awaited before creating `donePath`, so watchers can observe completion first and permanently report a successful background job as failed.

[Medium] src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/manager.mjs:2211 — The inverted guard disables cleanup whenever the server is idle and runs it during active work, allowing stale sessions/tombstones to accumulate in quiet processes.

[Medium] src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:465 — Normal settlement sets `settled=true` before calling this helper, so every progress-enabled foreground command leaks its interval and retained closure for process lifetime.

[Medium] src/runtime/shared/atomic-file.mjs:38 — The shortened loop removes the final rename attempt and instead sleeps after the last failed attempt before throwing, reducing transient-lock recovery while preserving its full delay.

Verdict: Not safe to merge; multiple correctness, security, and resource-management regressions remain.

HIGH — `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:516` — Interpolating `cwd` into shell text allows command substitution/metacharacters in valid directory names to execute outside policy scanning; pass `cwd` to `spawn` instead.

HIGH — `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/manager.mjs:2211` — Inverted guard disables cleanup while idle and runs it during active work, leaking stale sessions/tombstones and potentially aborting long-running sessions.

HIGH — `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/store.mjs:578` — Read/parse failures now become valid open sessions, allowing corrupt, unreadable, or concurrently deleted sessions to be re-saved as blank state and lose history.

MEDIUM — `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:727` — Exit-file write is not awaited before publishing `donePath`, so watchers can observe completion first and misclassify successful jobs as failed.

MEDIUM — `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:465` — `settle()` sets `settled` before calling this helper, preventing progress intervals from ever being cleared and leaking a timer/closure per completed command.

MEDIUM — `src/runtime/shared/atomic-file.mjs:38` — Final backoff now sleeps without a following rename attempt, reducing retry count and still incurring the full delay before failure.

Verdict: Reject; multiple security, data-loss, lifecycle, and resource-management regressions remain.
